body.title: Fix ENGRAM_CODE_* triggers to point at 'cells' payload key
body.result: ENGRAM_CODE_* trigger key now matches payload 'cells'; run_engram_cursor wrapper added; re-seeded; test passes
body.description: Two structural bugs make the engram coding chain dead-on-arrival: (1) seed_coding_engrams writes triggers={'__entry__': 'coding sprint entry'} but payload key is 'cells' — node_executor looks up payload.get('coding sprint entry'), finds nothing, logs WARN and no-ops silently (node_executor.py:136-146, seed_coding_engrams.py:118,121); (2) two habits (PROC_INVOKE_SPRINT, PROC_INVOKE_COMMIT) reference code_ref='pe_chain:run_engram_cursor' which doesn't exist in pe_chain.py. Fix shape: change seed to triggers={'__entry__': 'cells'} (match node_executor's convention), re-seed all ENGRAM_CODE_* rows, then add run_engram_cursor as thin wrapper in pe_chain.py that loads the entry engram and calls cursor_runtime.run_cursor. Scope: seed script + re-seed + thin wrapper in pe_chain.py. Does not register pe_* per-step functions as MCPCALL tools (separate ticket T-engram-mcpcall-register-pe-steps). Not HIGH-inertia. Test plan: seed a test engram in-memory, call execute_node, assert instructions_run > 0. No mocks — real DB.
